notes: New York-based internet commerce software company; created Commerce eXchange platform; went public (NASDAQ: IWTV); pivoted to B2B e-commerce space; merged with DynamicWeb Enterprises (April 2000) creating eB2B Commerce, Inc.; eB2B filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy (October 2004); Interworld brand dissolved.

Technology-savvy consumer software company (Carmen Sandiego, Myst); used InterWorld Digital Delivery for electronic software distribution; acquired by The Learning Company (1998); The Learning Company acquired by Mattel (1999); Mattel sold to Gores Group; operations eventually wound down.

Consumer electronics/computer direct retailer; used Commerce eXchange to sell 20,000+ computer products and distribute Microsoft software titles; acquired by PC Mall and subsequently by Insight Direct.
Direct retailer operating PC Zone and Mac Zone internet superstores; used Commerce eXchange for e-commerce; business continued as Zones, Inc.
IT equipment leasing company; used Commerce eXchange from 100+ worldwide locations to help 8,000 customers acquire/manage IT equipment; Comdisco filed bankruptcy (2001) during dot-com collapse.
Educational publisher; used Commerce eXchange for internet publishing systems for school systems in the US; still active as Scholastic Corporation (NASDAQ: SCHL) as of 2026.

Successor to InterWorld (via DynamicWeb merger 2000); also acquired Netlan Enterprises (2000) and Bac-Tech Systems (2002); filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy (October 2004); liquidated assets; Mandalay was post-bankruptcy entity pursuing different business lines.
